name: PREVAIL III Ebola Survivor Cohort
aliases: Partnership for Research on Ebola Virus in Liberia III
denominator_method: Ebola virus disease survivors (antibody-confirmed) and their close contacts enrolled in Liberia and followed longitudinally.
time_zero_definition: Enrolment after recovery from acute Ebola virus disease
funders: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID/NIH)
notes: Large observational natural-history cohort with a close-contact comparator, banked specimens, and up to 5 years of 6-monthly follow-up. Documented ocular (uveitis), musculoskeletal, neurologic and other sequelae plus viral persistence in immune-privileged sites. n_enrolled shown is the target survivor count; ~1,126 survivors enrolled by 2017 (plus ~2,700 contacts). Quantitative findings pending human extraction from source tables.

publications.title: A Longitudinal Study of Ebola Sequelae in Liberia

findings.outcome_verbatim: uveitis (ocular sequela) significantly more prevalent among survivors than close contacts
findings.source_locator: Results (uveitis among six symptoms significantly more common in survivors vs contacts; exact percentages pending extraction)
